my_thoughts: The leaky-jar frame did its job?provoked ridicule, which means they felt the sting. I need to hold the analogy steady and force them to name whether endless filling is miserable. Hypothesis: if they deny the misery explicitly, the analogy can pivot to show endless craving as punishment.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student scoffs at the image ('That?s a ridiculous image, Socrates')?resist_intellectually via pushes_back. They grasp the picture but reject its force. The structural_analogy (leaky jar) is meant to make appetite-without-end feel absurd. Pattern seed: mockery of analogy before substance. Confidence medium: pushback is expected when the analogy attacks their lifestyle.
visible_response: Yet it’s your way of life I’m describing, Callicles—endless craving, never satisfaction.
